hypothesis: A scalar reload_factor sweep might close the remaining baseline parity gap.
change_summary: Swept reload-only tuning ideas without changing the deeper branch-evolution semantics.

metrics.summary: No durable closure path emerged from scalar reload-only tuning.

do_not_repeat: Do not reopen reload-only scalar sweeps as the main parity strategy.
next_if_any: Move to branch-evolution experiments instead of more scalar scans.

hypothesis: Preserving reversal tangent near G0 would reduce period drift while keeping MRDF damping reduction behavior.
change_summary: Added experimental mode 3 branch law with reversal-tangent-preserving formulation and exact tangent handshake.
do_not_repeat: Do not rerun unchanged mode 3 as a candidate closure path.
next_if_any: Only revisit if a new F_mrdf or curvature rule materially changes the branch law.

hypothesis: Raising translated-branch tangent reference without changing tau anchoring could improve parity without destabilizing the branch.
change_summary: Added experimental mode 4 that keeps translated-local tau anchoring but partially restores tangent reference toward G0.
do_not_repeat: Do not treat unchanged mode 4 as the acceptance solution.
next_if_any: Use it as the current best non-accepting system-level baseline.

hypothesis: If lower layers dominate compliance, restricting tangent restore to bottom layers might move the system-period signature.
change_summary: Ran selective mode 4 variants on bottom 3 and bottom 2 layers only.
do_not_repeat: Do not rerun bottom-layer-only mode 4 variants without a new constitutive idea behind them.
next_if_any: Focus on branch-evolution semantics instead of layer selection.

family: single-element direct replay and branch tracing
hypothesis: Local replay against workbook-derived hysteresis can isolate whether the residual is path-level or system-assembly-level.
change_summary: Added constitutive replay utilities and compared local path behavior against Layer 1 workbook hysteresis.
do_not_repeat: Use this family for diagnosis only; it is not itself a closure strategy.
next_if_any: Pair it with all-layer diagnostics before trusting a local replay improvement.

hypothesis: Layer-by-layer tangent audit may show a dominant stiffness carrier that explains the unchanged period signature.
change_summary: Added all-layer sweep audit and compared current vs mode 4 compliance distributions and equivalent stiffness minima.
do_not_repeat: Do not treat layer dominance alone as the closure hypothesis.
next_if_any: Use all-layer sweep to support multi-layer path targeting, not isolated branch-law tuning.

family: branch-progress or reversal-state aware F_mrdf evolution
hypothesis: The remaining residual may come from overly static F_mrdf usage; branch-progress or reversal-state aware evolution could move the governing tangent history.
change_summary: Implemented experimental mode 5 that evolves F_mrdf from 1.0 at reversal toward the history-target factor over branch progress.
do_not_repeat: Do not rerun unchanged standalone mode 5 progression as a primary closure path.
next_if_any: Any future F_mrdf evolution work should be coupled to a multi-layer target, not retried standalone.

family: translated branch curvature, including hybrid curvature+tangent-restore
hypothesis: The remaining residual may be driven by translated-branch curvature rather than by standalone F_mrdf progression.
change_summary: Implemented experimental mode 6 translated curvature progression and experimental mode 7 translated curvature plus tangent restore.
do_not_repeat: Do not rerun unchanged mode 6 or mode 7 as primary closure paths.
next_if_any: Use this family only if tied to a stronger multi-layer target than Layer 1 replay quality.

hypothesis: The remaining elastic-halfspace gap might be explained by an explicit bedrock damping ratio such as 2 percent.
change_summary: Reviewed DeepSoil v7.1 halfspace documentation, added bedrock damping_ratio to config and web payloads for parity/reference, and verified GeoWave time-domain elastic-halfspace solvers remain impedance-only in behavior.

metrics.deepsoil_manual_note: Bedrock damping ratio has no effect in time domain analyses.

do_not_repeat: Do not treat standalone bedrock percent damping tuning as a primary closure path for time-domain elastic-halfspace parity.
next_if_any: If elastic-halfspace parity remains important, focus on impedance and input semantics rather than bedrock damping ratio.

family: rigid vs elastic-halfspace boundary semantics audit
hypothesis: The observed rigid-versus-elastic gap may come from a real boundary-condition effect, but the input application semantics must be separated first.
change_summary: Ran a targeted nonlinear Newmark audit on the 5-layer baseline geometry with explicit bedrock Vs=760 m/s, unit weight=25 kN/m3, damping_ratio=0.02 for three cases: rigid+outcrop, rigid+within, and elastic_halfspace+outcrop.
do_not_repeat: Do not interpret the full rigid+outcrop vs elastic_halfspace+outcrop gap as a pure boundary-only effect without separating input semantics first.
next_if_any: For elastic-halfspace parity work, compare impedance/boundary effects only after aligning within-vs-outcrop semantics.

hypothesis: The remaining elastic-halfspace effect may be explainable by how the incident base force and dashpot reaction combine through the impedance boundary.
change_summary: Added boundary-force audit output to the implicit Newmark solver and ran a Kocaeli-based elastic-halfspace audit to measure incident force, dashpot force, and net boundary traction directly.
do_not_repeat: Do not treat elastic-halfspace parity as a raw-force-amplification problem without first checking incident-force and dashpot cancellation.
next_if_any: If elastic-halfspace parity remains important, inspect frequency content and phase of the residual net boundary traction rather than bedrock damping or gross force magnitude.

hypothesis: The remaining elastic-halfspace effect should appear as a frequency-selective residual boundary traction rather than as a gross force-magnitude increase.
change_summary: Added FFT-based post-analysis for elastic halfspace boundary-force audits and evaluated the Kocaeli residual traction against the elastic-halfspace surface response.
do_not_repeat: Do not read elastic-halfspace behavior only from total-force RMS; keep the frequency-local residual traction picture in view.
next_if_any: If halfspace parity must be closed further, compare the residual traction frequency content against the rigid-within reference and then decide whether the remaining work belongs in boundary implementation or constitutive parity.

family: shared boundary-input semantics plus DeepSoil DB delta comparison
hypothesis: Locking rigid and elastic paths to the same incident-wave meaning should fix the directional boundary mismatch before any new constitutive retuning.
change_summary: Added a shared boundary-input semantic adapter, switched rigid-within and elastic-outcrop loading onto that contract, added reconstructed-vs-assembled boundary-force audit fields, ingested DeepSoil batch DB boundary surfaces, compared GeoWave and DeepSoil deltas in the same report shape, and added case-local surface/input amplification audit fields to isolate the remaining magnitude gap.
do_not_repeat: Do not use rigid+outcrop vs elastic_halfspace+outcrop as the acceptance pair, and do not reopen new constitutive mode3/4/5/6/7 tuning before the boundary-first magnitude gap is cleaner.
next_if_any: Keep the acceptance pair at elastic_halfspace+outcrop vs rigid+within, prioritize rigid-within stress/tangent audit next, and only then reopen broad constitutive family work.

family: case-by-case GeoWave vs DeepSoil profile truth audit under locked boundary semantics
hypothesis: After the directional gate passes, per-case profile truth comparison can separate pure boundary magnitude issues from constitutive stress/tangent issues.
change_summary: Compared GeoWave rigid-within and elastic-outcrop acceptance cases directly against the matching DeepSoil DB cases at surface-metric and profile-metric level, including gamma_max, profile PGA, displacement, and max stress ratio.
do_not_repeat: Do not assume the remaining boundary-first magnitude gap is purely an input-scaling problem, purely deepest-layer compliance, or simply low average stiffness once case-truth profile and proxy metrics are available.
next_if_any: Use the reusable rigid-within all-layer stress-path and tangent-history audit against DeepSoil as the main closure loop, now reading derived stress/secant proxies, tau-peak proxy, and low-end tangent ratios before opening another broad constitutive family or rewriting boundary semantics again.

hypothesis: The remaining residual is more likely driven by how constitutive evolution distributes across layers than by any isolated Layer 1 branch-shape improvement.
change_summary: Constitutive multi-layer target family defined, but currently paused while the boundary-first first-order magnitude gap is being closed.
do_not_repeat: Do not skip straight back to new branch-law families before this multi-layer target is defined or while boundary-first magnitude closure is still open.
next_if_any: Return to this family only after the elastic-vs-rigid boundary acceptance pair is directionally and materially cleaner.
